Defines an enumeration for IMU coordinate axes in the Pebble firmware.

# Purpose
This code is a C header file that defines an enumeration named `IMUCoordinateAxis`. The enumeration specifies three constants: `AXIS_X`, `AXIS_Y`, and `AXIS_Z`, which represent the positive directions along the X, Y, and Z axes, respectively. These constants are used to describe the orientation of an Inertial Measurement Unit (IMU) in relation to a watch, with `AXIS_X` pointing to the right, `AXIS_Y` pointing to the top, and `AXIS_Z` pointing vertically out of the watchface. The `#pragma once` directive is used to ensure the file is included only once in a single compilation.
# Data Structures

---
### IMUCoordinateAxis
- **Type**: ``enum``
- **Members**:
    - ``AXIS_X``: Represents the positive direction along the X axis, going toward the right of the watch.
    - ``AXIS_Y``: Represents the positive direction along the Y axis, going toward the top of the watch.
    - ``AXIS_Z``: Represents the positive direction along the Z axis, going vertically out of the watchface.
- **Description**: Defines the coordinate axes for an IMU (Inertial Measurement Unit) in relation to a watch, specifying the positive directions for the X, Y, and Z axes.
